purpose: Whole-app CEO truth for feature claims, proof status, blockers, and audit freshness. This registry is deliberately honest: registry presence is not implementation proof.

features.name: Setup, install, and onboarding
features.user_contract: A new user can install memd, run first-use onboarding, and reach a working local proof without hand-editing hidden state.
allowed_claims: Setup/install/onboarding slice has local 25-star implementation proof; external validation remains pending.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim public 25/25, universal one-command install, production onboarding, or external validation until external replay passes.
features.freshness_policy: Re-run feature setup/install/onboarding proof after installer, CLI bootstrap, setup docs, README/START-HERE, update/uninstall scripts, or release changes.

features.name: Docs and product education
features.user_contract: Docs tell users what memd does, what is proven, what is aspirational, and where to start without conflicting claims.
allowed_claims: Registry defines current whole-app truth fields.
Docs/product education has strong local proof for navigation, start-here/README/setup/CLI alignment, setup examples, claim-to-proof mapping, and honest pending language when the proof script passes.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim docs prove product behavior without linked executable proof.
Do not claim external validation, dogfood readiness, production readiness, or whole-product 25/25 from local docs proof alone.
features.freshness_policy: Update registry, coverage report, FEATURES row, and product education proof whenever beginner navigation, README/START-HERE/setup docs, CLI setup/help surfaces, feature claims, proof artifacts, or status language changes.

features.name: Doctor, status, recovery, update, and uninstall
features.user_contract: A user can inspect health, understand failures, recover safely, update, and uninstall without losing data unexpectedly.
proof_artifacts: local temp proof summary emitted by scripts/verify/feature-doctor-status-recovery-update-uninstall-proof.sh
allowed_claims: Doctor/status/update/uninstall recovery slice has local executable proof for current repository surfaces.
Update and uninstall dry-runs state that .memd memory is preserved by default.
Doctor repair can recover generated bundle env files without deleting seeded memory/state sentinel files in the local proof.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim external verification until an independent replay is recorded.
Do not claim destructive reset safety or complete package-manager uninstall coverage until those contracts exist and are proven.
Do not claim all real-world corruptions are recoverable from this local generated-file recovery proof.
features.freshness_policy: Re-run the lifecycle proof after any doctor/status, setup bundle layout, .memd storage, update, uninstall, reset, or recovery behavior change; add external replay before unblocking 25/25.

features.name: Memory capture, lookup, recall, corrections, provenance, and trust
features.user_contract: Users can capture memories, retrieve them accurately, correct them, and inspect provenance/trust boundaries.
proof_commands: bash scripts/memd-cargo-guard.sh check -p memd-client -p memd-core -p memd-schema
allowed_claims: Memory-core substrate surfaces have current local proof coverage across capture, lookup, resume, corrections, provenance, and trust boundaries.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim reliable memory OS, perfect recall, production-grade trust, or external verification from this local proof artifact alone.
features.freshness_policy: Re-run feature-memory-core-proof plus guarded memory cargo check/tests after ingest, store, lookup, resume, correction, provenance, schema, or trust-boundary changes.

features.name: Context compiler and token savings
features.user_contract: memd compiles useful compact context and reports honest token savings without hiding critical facts.
allowed_claims: Local context compiler/token-savings proof has a reproducible fixture ledger with positive saved tokens, quality retention, and budget enforcement.
Historical in-repository V11/V15 compiler/token proof scripts and dated artifacts are cited as supporting local evidence.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim externally verified token savings until an independent/public replay artifact is registered.
Do not publish broad product-wide savings percentages beyond the local fixture and cited historical artifacts.
features.freshness_policy: Refresh measurements after compiler, prompt, tokenizer, benchmark corpus, budget policy, or quality rubric changes; keep external verification pending until independent replay lands.

features.name: Shared research cache and donor repo extraction
features.user_contract: Teams can reuse extracted research context across repos while preserving source attribution and avoiding cross-project contamination.
allowed_claims: The implemented local inspiration/research lane search cache has strong executable local proof for cold miss and warm hit behavior.
Local proof shows source attribution fields and rendered file/line/section metadata for inspiration search hits.
Local proof shows cache invalidation on allowlisted file content fingerprint changes.
Local proof shows root/query/limit isolation, fixed inspiration-lane allowlisting, and no bleed of a non-allowlisted private fixture sentinel into hits, renders, or cache artifacts.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim production-safe shared research cache, complete RAG cache safety, complete donor repo extraction, dogfood validation, external verification, or contamination-free/private-data-safe arbitrary multi-repo sharing from this local proof alone.
Do not claim external verification until an independent replay is recorded.
features.freshness_policy: Re-run proof after changes to inspiration search cache keys, lane file allowlists, fingerprint fields, RAG integration, donor extraction, cache formats, or multi-repo research workflows.

features.name: Hive and hivemind coordination
features.user_contract: Multiple agents can coordinate through memd with clear ownership, durable state, and replayable handoffs.
allowed_claims: A current strong local proof validates hive coordination contracts, executable script modes, live-map separation guards, an isolated hive production rehearsal, and archived ad hoc artifacts for roster, authority, capability, handoff, task ownership, help/review lanes, dev-server conflict receipts, and claim release.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim sustained production hive/hivemind reliability, continuous dogfood, external verification, optional shared/Tailscale canary coverage, or cross-agent leakage/private-context sharing from this local proof.
features.freshness_policy: Re-run this proof after hive coordination, authority, capability, live-map, handoff, task-board, receipt, or guard-script mode changes; rerun the full hive production proof and add external/shared canary evidence before upgrading dogfood or external status.

features.name: Competitor and public benchmark replay
features.user_contract: Benchmark comparisons can be replayed from public fixtures with clear baselines and no private-only assumptions.
proof_artifacts: docs/verification/25-5-memory-os-runs/YYYY-MM-DD-competitor-head-to-head.json (optional; only current if present and passing)
allowed_claims: Local public fixture replay has strong deterministic proof when the feature proof command passes: fixture checksums, lexical baseline rows, memd rows, item limits, and metric bounds are validated.
External live replay remains planned; same-fixture competitor comparison is current only when a fresh passing competitor replay artifact exists.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim competitor superiority, market leadership, benchmark wins, or external verification from local fixture replay alone.
Do not reuse stale competitor artifacts as current benchmark proof.
Do not cite dynamic ports, runtime durations, or local mini-fixture replay as independent third-party benchmark evidence.
features.freshness_policy: Refresh after benchmark fixture, checksum, baseline, competitor version, scoring, replay-script, or claim-boundary changes; dated artifacts must be same-day or one-day-fresh.

features.name: Dogfood reliability windows
features.user_contract: The team can demonstrate memd working over sustained real use windows, with failures logged and recovery measured.
allowed_claims: Local proof can inspect dated dogfood/reliability artifacts and report whether spans are ad hoc or sustained.
Current checkout contains dated ad hoc dogfood evidence, but no closed sustained reliability window is proven.
Strong local proof computes deterministic artifact inventory, dated evidence, and explicit sustained-window absence/presence without upgrading dogfood status unless the sustained-window rule passes.
Current checkout has dated ad hoc evidence and zero sustained windows under the no-false-positive rule; dogfood status remains ad_hoc.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim sustained or continuous dogfood until dated closed window artifacts/logs prove actual real-use duration and failure/recovery review.
Do not claim external validation, production reliability, or 25/25 dogfood readiness from local repository proof alone.
features.freshness_policy: Re-run after changes to dogfood enrollment/status, installer/setup flows, memory storage, server/runtime reliability, release process, or whenever a new dogfood window starts/closes; commit refreshed deterministic summary artifacts with any claimed proof update.

features.name: External replay and auditor proof
features.user_contract: An outside auditor can replay proofs and reach the same status conclusions from public instructions and artifacts.
allowed_claims: External replay/auditor readiness has strong local proof: registry/schema wiring, verifier instructions, artifact immutability/checksums, registered public-smoke report schema, and fresh 25-5-external-public-smoke generation/validation are checked without leaving dirty proof noise.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim externally verified, independently replayed, auditor-approved, or whole-product 25/25 until an independent external replay/auditor artifact is recorded.
features.freshness_policy: Re-run the local readiness proof after every verifier instruction, registry/schema, release bundle, public replay runner, or external-public-smoke artifact change; keep external_status planned until independent external evidence exists.

features.name: Product UX surfaces, dashboard, and CLI language
features.user_contract: Users see clear, consistent language in CLI/dashboard surfaces and understand next actions without reading source code.
allowed_claims: Local docs, CLI help, and dashboard source language have current text-inspection proof for setup discoverability and vocabulary consistency.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim polished product UX, complete dashboard, browser-verified walkthrough, accessibility compliance, external validation, or 25/25 readiness from this local text proof alone.
features.freshness_policy: Re-run this proof after every CLI, dashboard, status, docs, setup, or error-message language change; add browser walkthrough/external evidence before unblocking 25/25.

features.name: Network identity, federation, and market layer
features.user_contract: Networked identities and federation/market behavior are explicit, safe, and not confused with local-only memory behavior.
allowed_claims: Local proof verifies a single user/org local memory identity/control plane across Codex, Hermes, and OpenClaw/Claude-style harness surfaces.
Existing federation/market evidence is limited to local/synthetic V17 routine marketplace proof plus planned V26/V27/V28 gates when no newer proof artifacts are present.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim active network identity service, cross-org federation, public marketplace, billing/market economics, production federation safety, or external verification from this local proof alone.
features.freshness_policy: Run the feature proof and registry audit before any public claim or code path involving network identity, federation, billing, or markets; add dated V26/V27/V28 artifacts before expanding claims.

features.name: Release and claim honesty gates
features.user_contract: Release claims are limited to what registry status and proof artifacts support.
allowed_claims: Local release-claim honesty proof checks the registry audit, release checklist hooks, executable proof commands, and unsupported 25/25 overclaim blockers.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim 25/25, production-ready release gating, release-flow integration, or external verification from this local honesty proof alone.
features.freshness_policy: Run registry audit and the local release-claim honesty proof before release notes, README claim changes, scorecard changes, public benchmark updates, tags, or announcements.

features.name: Cross-harness continuity
features.user_contract: Continuity survives across CLI, server, agent, benchmark, and external replay harnesses with equivalent state interpretation.
allowed_claims: Current local proof validates documented continuity surfaces across available Codex, Hermes, OpenClaw, and Claude-style harness packs, including shared wake/memory/event bundle surfaces, wake/resume/write-path parity, and existing local process replay artifact sanity checks when present.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim seamless cross-harness continuity, production-grade continuity, real external cross-session continuity, or independent external verification from this local/ad hoc proof alone.
features.freshness_policy: Re-run feature-cross-harness-continuity-proof after state schema, CLI/server protocol, import/export, handoff/resume/wake, generated harness pack, or integration doc changes; re-run the process replay before upgrading beyond local/ad hoc status.
